title: 今年戛纳，台上大骂“Fuck AI” | 原文
content: At the 2024 Cannes Film Festival, director Guillermo del Toro shocked the audience by yanking onto the microphone during a pre‑premiere event, loudly declaring “Fuck AI.” The remark was delivered immediately after a 4K restoration screening and was met with stunned silence followed by laughter and applause. Cannes’ artistic director responded by amplifying the statement into an official stance against certain uses of artificial intelligence. The incident highlighted growing controversy over AI’s role in filmmaking.

title: 想搞 AI 的小老板是真离谱
content: The author recounts an interview with a small, startup‑like company—essentially a new business arm of a large group—whose owner, around 30 years old, seeks to develop AI applications. During the meeting the owner, who appears to be the technical decision‑maker, asks impractical questions about deploying trillion‑parameter models, revealing a lack of technical understanding. The author responds that for the company’s stage, simply using APIs suffices and that even a 70‑B model fine‑tuning would be unnecessary within a year. The owner’s focus on tea sets rather than office decor underscores his whimsical, unrealistic outlook.

title: 20 年原生 iOS 开发者的反思：为什么复杂文本处理最终还是要靠 Electron？
content: A veteran Swift developer tried building a Markdown‑enabled chat app purely with native macOS technologies, but encountered performance, rendering, and UI limitations. TextKit, SwiftUI, and AppKit approaches failed to provide smooth, scrollable, richly formatted text. Switching to WebKit, and ultimately an Electron project, solved the problems instantly, delivering reliable Markdown rendering, smooth scrolling, and built‑in text controls. The experience shows that for complex, long‑form text the native SDKs are inadequate, while Web technologies provide immediate, high‑performance solutions.

They examine the challenges of deploying technology at scale, the importance of on‑the‑beat experience, and how policing skills will evolve over the next decade.

content: The article outlines five levels of research for investment: 1) company analysis, 2) industry analysis, 3) era analysis, 4) studying experts, and 5) self‑analysis. Company focus can be misleading (e.g., Moutai’s returns), while industry and era studies reveal broader trends such as AI’s impact or demographic shifts. Learning from credible experts offers robust, tested views, and understanding one’s own traits, risk tolerance, and resources helps tailor a suitable investing strategy.

content: Fudan University associate professor Shen Yifei faced a backlash after a livestream with a parent’s child claimed “school bullying” over a snacks incident. Shifting normal peer conflict into alleged bullying, Shen labeled it a victim‑hood mistake. The parent retaliated with privacy accusations and reported Shen to university authorities, forcing her to detail her actions and cooperate with investigations. The incident highlights overused complaint mechanisms that pressure teachers, eroding their enthusiasm and sparking debates about the balance of accountability in education.

title: 武汉大学取消预约入校后，学生在食堂无座可坐，有游客爬树拍照，如何调和校园开放与教学生活秩序间的冲突？
content: After Wuhan University lifted its visitor reservation system on May 13, students reported crowding in cafeterias, lack of seating, and tourists climbing trees to take photos or livestream classes. Some visitors streamed athletes during workouts, violating campus etiquette. Students expressed mixed feelings: many welcomed the open campus, while others warned of safety risks and classroom disruptions, calling for stricter rules. The university explained that the dining surge on May 16–17 was due to a spike in high‑school visitors after exams, and said police lacked enforcement power over unruly tourists, urging students to file complaints through campus channels.

title: 周五三国长债大跌的一点思考
content: In late June, 30‑year Treasury yields spiked simultaneously in the US, Japan, and the UK—record highs that have not been seen since 2007‑1998. This reflects a shift in global credit, driven by higher commodity costs, supply disruptions, rising inflation, and a weakening U.S. dollar due to reduced foreign demand. Rising fiscal deficits and debt‑financing pressures cause long‑term rates to rise, limiting central banks’ ability to buy bonds without triggering currency and inflation concerns.

title: Cannibalistic attacks between gray seals leave telltale “corkscrew” injuries
content: Gray seals sometimes engage in cannibalistic fights that produce distinctive “corkscrew” lacerations on their bodies. These spiral wounds—created when one seal’s claws twist into another’s skin—serve as evidence of aggressive inter‑specific interactions. Researchers use the characteristic shape and placement of the injuries to identify the occurrence of these attacks and study the social dynamics and territorial disputes within seal populations.

title: Jank now has its own custom IR
content: Jank, the game engine, has introduced its own custom intermediate representation (IR), allowing developers to manipulate code at a lower level before it is compiled into native binaries. This IR layer provides a more flexible and expressive way to optimize performance and supports backends for different CPUs and GPUs. The move is intended to improve toolchain modularity and enable advanced features such as fine‑grained caching and reusable optimization passes.

title: 国产的 AI IDE 矮子头上选将军好像也就 TRAE 好用点
content: Most non‑technical programmers find China’s AI IDE “TRAE” the easiest to use. Earlier tools like Cursor are becoming less convenient—for example, customizing model URLs now requires a paid plan. Updated options such as Qoder feel better but lack enough credits, and their custom‑model feature works only with preset providers. TRAE offers more openness, allowing users to set a Base URL; however, alternatives like CodeX or ChatGPT Pro are difficult to access without a domestic VISA card.

title: “天崩开局”的国产电影变成黑马，潮汕文旅迎来泼天富贵？
content: The low‑budget film *“Letter to Grandma”* has surprised China’s film and tourism sectors. Made for 14 million yuan, it hit 300 million yuan in less than a month, triggering a surge in Shantou tourism, hotel bookings, and museum visits—up 200 %—and prompting local governments to launch ticket‑stub discounts to boost spending. The movie’s emotional story of overseas Chinese mail ties into a broader trend: culturally resonant content drives real‑world tourism, as seen with the game *Shenmu: Monkey King* boosting visits to Shanxi historic sites.

title: 这条芯片赛道，彻底火了
content: AI chips are shifting from GPUs to ASICs as inference dominates demand. By 2026, ASICs could outpace GPUs, supported by orders from Google, OpenAI, Meta, and AWS. Major players—Broadcom, Marvell, MediaTek, Qualcomm—are expanding ASIC lines, while Chinese firms like Chipyuan and ASR grow as design‑service leaders. Economically, ASICs offer lower cost and energy per inference, compelling both cloud providers and hardware makers to adopt custom silicon, reshaping the AI infrastructure market.

title: 沃集鲜翻红，拯救大卖场
content: Walmart’s “Woji Xian” private‑brand, launched in China in 2019, has revitalised the retailer by offering low‑priced, well‑packaged staples that mimic Sam’s Club quality. The brand leverages Walmart’s global supply chain, focuses on simple ingredients and local sourcing, and is sold in small, community‑shop formats. This strategy targets middle‑class single households and small families that Sam’s Club overlooks, helping Walmart survive the decline of large‑format stores and capture a growing private‑label market.

title: 一个月狂烧130万美元，龙虾之父自曝token账单，费用OpenAI全包

Despite the expense, he claims the work is covered by OpenAI’s support and highlights how token usage is becoming a new metric for AI-driven productivity.

content: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation no longer holds Microsoft shares. On Friday, its Gates Foundation Trust sold the remaining 7.7 million Microsoft shares, worth about $3.2 billion at current prices. Gates himself still owns 103 million Microsoft shares, valued at $43 billion.

content: ASML and Tata Electronics have signed an agreement to boost India’s semiconductor production. ASML’s technology will help Tata build a 300‑mm wafer fab in Gujarat, a US$11 billion investment aimed at making chips for automotive, mobile devices and AI. The partnership aligns with India’s goal of expanding local chip manufacturing and will also collaborate on talent development, supply chain expansion and R&D infrastructure. The MoU was signed during PM Modi’s visit to the Netherlands.

title: VoIP brings back old-fashioned pay phones to rural Vermont (2025)
content: Patrick Schlott, an electrical engineer in Vermont, restores old pay phone hardware and connects it to modern VoIP gateways, creating free-to-use public phones. Seven units now sit in libraries, schools, a town hall, and community centers, providing coinless calls across the U.S. and Canada. The project fills a gap as Vermont will ban smartphones in schools in 2026, and many rural areas lack cell service. Schlott funds the work through donations and personal money, keeping the phones publicly accessible for essential communications.

title: 横店短剧大撤退：停工、降薪，与被挤掉的饭碗 | 原文
content: Short‑drama production in Hengdian has sharply declined, with many crews halted and directors facing lower pay or being cut from jobs. After a month of reopening, director Li Yunming received few offers, all demanding intensive shooting schedules—four days for three 30‑episode series—for only ¥1,500‑¥2,000 per day. Concerned about workload and hours, he declined most projects. In early March, an old‑partner team secured a contract for a local project; to close the deal, Li cut his fee by 20% despite tight budgets.

title: Gemma 4 vs Claude vs Llama: Which Model Wins for Devs
content: Gemma 4, released April 2026, is a family of models (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) that can run on everything from a Raspberry Pi to a single GPU. It scores far above previous releases on math, coding, and tool‑use benchmarks, without price or usage limits, and is open‑licensed under Apache 2.0. Claude offers higher raw reasoning in API form but is closed and costly; Llama 4 provides large context windows but has license restrictions and weaker coding performance. For developers needing ownership, low cost, and competitive performance, Gemma 4 is the most flexible choice.
